notes_text: <!-- Generated by clean_summary.py -- <!-- Source: meeting_notes_2026-03-17_regular.md -- <!-- Generated: 2026-03-27 04:01 UTC -- Conroe ISD Board Meeting — 2026-03-17 Regular Meeting — Unofficial Notes Based on auto-generated transcript. Not an official record. Generated 2026-03-27 03:56 UTC Unofficial Meeting Notes Conroe Independent School District Board of Trustees Regular Meeting — March 17, 2026 Note: These are unofficial notes based on an auto-generated transcript and may contain errors or omissions. For official records, please consult the district's approved minutes. Meeting Overview - Called to Order: 5:02 PM by Board President Odenweller - Adjourned: 10:56 PM - Quorum: Present 7 board members referenced throughout via 7-0 votes - Location: Not at the regular administration office; the board noted the next two meetings would return to the administration office while the current facility is used for end-of-year student events. Procedural Changes Announced President Odenweller announced several structural changes to board meetings going forward: 1. Closed session moved to 5:00 PM — to be completed before the 6:00 PM public session, so community members are not left waiting. 2. Modified special recognitions — Students/families will receive certificates at a small reception prior to the public meeting; achievements will still be acknowledged publicly during the meeting. 3. Streamlined principal/leadership appointments — Approved through the consent agenda human resources report rather than individual votes during the meeting. Closed Session - Entered: 5:08 PM - Returned to Open Session: 6:31 PM - Sections Cited: Texas Government Code §551.076, §551.071, §551.074 Action Taken: - Motion: To uphold the Level 1 and Level 2 grievance decision and authorize the board president to issue a written determination. - Vote: Approved 7-0 Special Recognitions Caney Creek High School Co-Ed Cheer — 2026 USA National Champions - Competed in Anaheim, California at USA Spirit National Championships - Scored 98.6 in finals; also received silver medal in best overall fight song - Coaches: Jaylen Jackson head coach , Chitty Noazi name unverified , Kathy Peara name unverified , Peggy Lions name unverified - Principal Dr. Jeffrey Stichler present - President Odenweller gave personal remarks noting she had watched the program grow College Park High School Cheer — 2026 UCA Division 1 National Champions - Competed in Orlando, Florida in the UCA Division 1 National High School Championships Super Large Division 1 Game Day - Received automatic placement in finals after Day 1; won national championship on Day 3 - Coaches: Abby Constantino name unverified , Maggie Meadow name unverified , Chandler Howard name unverified - Principal Robert MacFarlane present - President Odenweller noted both College Park and Caney Creek cheer squads support each other, calling it a "coast to coast national championship" for the district Claire Giden name unverified — 2026 UIL Girls Wrestling State Champion The Woodlands HS - 155-pound junior; won all matches by pin or technical fall except at district - Coach: William Durning name unverified - Principal Dennis Muehsler present - Trustee recognition noted Claire is a neighbor and her family's dedication was praised College Park High School Wrestling — 2026 UIL State Champions - District champions 8th year in a row; dual state champions 2nd year in a row; 2nd place team at UIL state tournament - Coaches: Eric Sput name unverified , Lindsay Spute name unverified , Kenneth Mark name unverified , Brad Ewing name unverified - Individual State Champions: - Mason Milsaps — 132 lbs, freshman, 40-4 record, two-time US Open national champion, Pan-American champion - Landry von Gotton name unverified — 135 lbs, junior, 42-1 record, third state championship, multi-time national champion, will represent Team USA at Pan-American Games - Logan Milsaps — 144 lbs, senior, 38-3 record, two-time state champion, team captain, 4.3 GPA, committed to wrestle at Arkansas Little Rock Micah Tennyson name unverified — 2026 UIL Freestyle Swimming State Champion Oak Ridge HS - Junior; won both 100 and 200 freestyle at state - Holds school records in 100, 200, and 500 freestyle - Coaches: Christine Ross name unverified head , Jeff Spoon name unverified assistant - Principal Shawn Almond present The Woodlands High School Girls Swimming — 2026 UIL 6A State Champions - 13th state title ; won the meet before the final event - Coaches: Jeremy Wade name unverified head , Kevin Witt, Marty Miller name unverified , Dylan Venter name unverified - Special announcement: Brooke Miller name unverified named TGCA Swimming Athlete of the Year - Principal Dennis Muehsler present Citizen Participation Public Comment Palin Lackey name unverified Freshman, Grand Oaks HS - Advocated for female flag football to become a UIL sport - Cited safety concerns with tackle football 176,831 female athletes in emergency rooms over 10 years - Noted the Houston Texans and Dallas Cowboys are willing to help sponsor school programs - Asked the board and Dr. Vinson for formal support to take to the state for UIL approval Mark Brooks name unverified - Thanked the board for discussing library material reviews in open session last month - Raised concerns about the library material challenge process : - Challenge forms were not released to the public; he obtained them via public information request - The challenge form for Ann of West Philly included justifications about content lesbians, drunkenness that were not discussed in open session - Argued discussions appeared based on feelings rather than local policy EFB criteria - Requested the board vote to establish a library advisory council at the next meeting Kristen Brown name unverified Parent, Oak Ridge feeder - Supported thoughtful discussion on library book challenges - Expressed concern that the board packet 590 pages was not available until approximately 3:00 PM the day of the meeting - Noted the January meeting packet wasn't posted before the meeting started - Asked the board and Dr. Vinson to ensure packets are available earlier for public review Michelle Taylor name unverified Retired CISD math teacher, 21 years - Referenced Dr. Jared Horath name unverified neurocognitive scientist who testified before the US Senate that Generation Z is the first not to outscore the previous generation on cognitive tests, attributing this to overuse of technology - Noted Sweden is spending hundreds of millions to return to textbooks - Shared personal concerns about her 10-year-old grandson with dysgraphia, ADHD, and anxiety struggling with computer-based tests, particularly in math - Requested the district move students off computers and back to textbooks and paper tests , starting with math Consent Agenda Items A–J - Vote: Approved 7-0 as a single motion - No items were removed for separate consideration - Note: Principal appointments were included in the consent agenda under the human resources report a new streamlined process Human Resources — Principal Appointments All appointments were approved as part of the consent agenda. The following were publicly introduced and celebrated: Elementary Principals Bradley Elementary — Tracy Somerville name unverified - Replacing Dr. Christine Butler retiring after career in CISD since 1999; opened two schools - Nearly two decades in public education, mostly in CISD - Currently assistant principal at Hines Elementary; previously AP at Wilkinson Elementary 10 years and district literacy coach Suchma Elementary — Nick Crawl name unverified - Replacing Jamie Almond moved to new Casey Arnold Elementary - Background in Pflugerville ISD and CISD; taught elementary for 9 years - Currently assistant principal at Gordon-Reed Elementary - Celebrated his 50th birthday at the meeting Intermediate/Junior High Principals Cox Intermediate — Valentina Majeski name unverified - Replacing Liz Koehler resigning to stay home with family - 7 years
